task_completion.description: Agent locates and selects the 'TumorSegmentation' node within the Slicer interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'TumorSegmentation' item is highlighted or selected in the Data module list or Segmentations module list.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the export interface for the segmentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export/Import models and labelmaps' section is expanded in the Segmentations module, OR a right-click context menu on the segmentation shows 'Export', OR the 'Save Data' dialog is open.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the output format to be STL.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown menu shows 'STL' or 'Stereolithography' selected, or the filename extension '.stl' is visible in a text field.

task_completion.description: Agent specifies the correct destination path and filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'tumor_model.stl' is visible in the save dialog. The target folder 'Exports' (or full path 'Documents/SlicerData/Exports') is visible in the address bar or folder tree.
The agent clicks an 'Export', 'Save', or 'OK' button in the export/save dialog to commit the file creation.

integrity.description: Agent uses the 3D Slicer Graphical User Interface modules (Data or Segmentations) rather than scripting entirely in the Python console.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Slicer's buttons, menus, and panels (e.g., Segmentations module panel), not primarily typing code into the Python console.

integrity.description: Agent does not use the operating system terminal to copy or generate the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No command line or terminal windows are used to copy, move, or generate the STL file.

integrity.description: The workflow appears to be a genuine interaction with the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots shows a logical progression of opening menus and dialogs, rather than the result suddenly appearing without interface interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is the BraTS 2021 Brain Tumor Segmentation Challenge (Case BraTS2021_00000). The specific segmentation node to be exported is named 'TumorSegmentation'. The required output format is STL (Stereolithography).
